title: lithocarpus brevicaudatus
intro: Lithocarpus brevicaudatus, also known as the Burford holly oak, is a plant species in the beech family Fagaceae. Traditionally, Lithocarpus brevicaudatus has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating coughs, and is also used to treat respiratory issues and wounds. Lithocarpus brevicaudatus is often used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water.
